text:
Faith Kipyegon's silver medal from the 5,000m final in Paris has been reinstated after the Kenyan runner was initially disqualified.

Kipyegon narrowly missed out on gold, having finished behind compatriot Beatrice Chebet in the final few metres of the race.

Chebet was too strong for her countrywoman in the final straight, with the Kenyan kicking home to top the podium inside a time of 15 minutes.

But following the race's conclusion, Kipyegon - who thought she'd done enough for at least a silver medal - learned her fate had already been decided.

Adjudged to have obstructed the track two laps from the end, Kipyegon was disqualified by the race referees, with third-placed Sifan Hassan moved up to silver and Italy’s Nadia Battocletti the new bronze medalist.

Gudaf Tsegay was the woman at the heart of it all, with Kipyegon said to have jostled with the Ethiopian, who eventually finished the race in ninth.

But the drama didn't stop there. Unsurprisingly, Kipyegon and Athletics Kenya were quick to lodge an appeal, which they discovered had been successful long after the race finished.

It meant Kenya took home a one-two finish in the women's 5,000-metre race, with Chebet's gold the first for the athletics-loving nation on the track at this year's Games.

Official women’s 5000m Podium finish.???????????? Beatrice Chebet ?????????????????? Faith Kipyegon ?????????????????? Sifan Hassan ????????????#Paris2024#OlympicGamesParis2024pic.twitter.com/7keSfxTsao



Speaking after the race, bronze medalist and Hassan revealed she was pleased Kipyegon had been reinstated, even if it denied the Dutch runner a silver medal.

The 31-year-old said: "I’m really happy Faith was reinstated. She’s a phenomenal athlete and I have a lot of respect for her.

"I would have been proud even if I was fourth. It was an incredible field, and I love my bronze medal.”

It's unlikely to be much consolation for reigning European champion Battocletti, who just missed out on the podium, despite setting an Italian record on the Parisian track.

Don't miss:Tom Daley gave diving partner Noah Williams X-rated gift

It wasn't the only medal drama from Day 10 of the Paris Games, with the women's floor final in the gymnastics also marred by controversy.

Ana Barbosu of Romania appeared to have finished third, only for a late appeal from Team USA gymnast Jordan Chilesbumping the Romanian down to fourth instead.

The Romanian team were left particularly frustrated with the judges, after an appeal of their own was rejected.

Sabrina Voinea thought she'd done enough to squeeze onto the podium, but a score of 13.700 placed her fifth.

Despite submitting a score review, the decision was upheld, meaning Team Romania went home empty-handed.

title: Olympic silver medallist wins appeal after being disqualified for nudging opponent | Other | Sport | Express.co.uk
author: Amos Murphy
description: Kenya's Faith Kipyegon had been stripped of her silver medal during a dramatic night in the Stade de France.
